steps.title: Translate each game into an equation
steps.content:
Each coin is worth $c$ points and each star is worth $s$ points, so total score = (coins)($c$) + (stars)($s$).

Game 1: 18 coins and 9 stars for 576 points gives $18c + 9s = 576$.

Game 2: 23 coins and 11 stars for 716 points gives $23c + 11s = 716$.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
The correct system must match $18c + 9s = 576$ and $23c + 11s = 716$.
  • A. $9c + 18s = 576$ swaps the coin and star counts; eliminated.
  • B. $18c + 9s = 576$ and $23c + 11s = 716$ matches both games exactly.
  • C. Places 576 and 716 as coefficients of $s$, which is dimensionally wrong; eliminated.
  • D. Places 576 and 716 as coefficients of $c$ with 9 and 11 on the right side; eliminated.
B is correct.
